title: quercus lanuginosa var. sulcata
intro: Quercus lanuginosa var. sulcata, also known as the sulcate corkbark oak, is a variety of evergreen tree or shrub native to the mountains of China. The sulcate corkbark oak is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of the sulcate corkbark oak include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing protection against certain diseases due to its antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of the sulcate corkbark oak typically involve drying and grinding the bark, which can be made into teas, capsules, or tinctures for medicinal use.
